description: Critical bug fixes, race condition elimination, reliability hardening, and polish for production-quality stability

userStories.title: Fix spinner ticker goroutine double-close panic
userStories.description: As a user, I need the spinner ticker goroutine to never panic from a double-close on the stopTicker channel.
acceptanceCriteria: In agent.go Run(), replace the raw 'stopTicker' channel with a sync.Once-guarded close pattern to prevent double-close panics
The stopTicker channel close at line ~326 (on first text token) and the fallback close at line ~344 must both be safe to call regardless of order
Remove the select/default pattern around the second close and use sync.Once instead
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Serialize concurrent tool output display
userStories.description: As a user, I need tool call output to not interleave when multiple tools run concurrently.
acceptanceCriteria: In agent.go executeTool(), wrap the ui.PrintToolCall() and ui.PrintToolResult()/ui.PrintToolError() calls in a shared mutex so concurrent tool goroutines don't interleave terminal output
Use the existing permMu mutex or add a dedicated displayMu sync.Mutex field on Agent
Permission prompts (which already hold permMu) should not deadlock with the display mutex
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add cancellable context to piped input handler
userStories.description: As a user, I need to be able to Ctrl+C out of piped input operations.
acceptanceCriteria: In handlePipedInput in cmd/root.go, replace context.Background() with a context that listens for SIGINT/SIGTERM
Use signal.NotifyContext(context.Background(), os.Interrupt, syscall.SIGTERM) to create the cancellable context
Pass this context to ag.Run() so the user can cancel piped operations
Add 'syscall' to imports if not already present
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Fix search_web ignoring http.NewRequest error
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the search_web tool to properly handle the http.NewRequest error instead of ignoring it.
acceptanceCriteria: In internal/tools/fetch.go ToolSearchWeb(), change 'req, _ := http.NewRequest(...)' to 'req, err := http.NewRequest(...)'
If err is not nil, return an error: 'return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to create search request: %v", err)'
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add atomic session save to prevent corruption
userStories.description: As a user, I need session saves to be atomic so a crash during save doesn't corrupt my session file.
acceptanceCriteria: In internal/session/session.go Save(), write to a temporary file first (same directory, .tmp suffix), then rename it to the final path
Use os.Rename() which is atomic on most filesystems
If the temp write fails, return the error without leaving a partial file
If os.Rename fails, attempt to clean up the temp file
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Cache token estimation to avoid O(n) per turn
userStories.description: As a user, I need long conversations to stay responsive by not re-estimating all message tokens every turn.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a 'cachedContextTokens int' and 'cachedMsgCount int' field to the Agent struct in agent.go
In the Run() loop where tokens.EstimateMessages is called, check if len(a.messages) == a.cachedMsgCount; if so, use the cached value
When new messages are appended, only estimate tokens for the new messages and add to the cached total
When messages are pruned (CompactToTokens), reset the cache by calling tokens.EstimateMessages on the remaining messages
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add grep context lines support (-B/-A flags)
userStories.description: As a user, I need the grep tool to support showing context lines around matches, like grep -B and -A.
acceptanceCriteria: Add 'before' and 'after' optional string parameters to the grep tool definition in tools.Definitions() with descriptions 'Number of lines to show before each match' and 'Number of lines to show after each match'
In execGrep in tools.go, parse before/after as integers (default 0)
When before or after > 0, include the surrounding lines in the output with '--' separators between non-contiguous groups
Cap before and after at 10 lines each to prevent excessive output
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add concurrent tool goroutine pool limit
userStories.description: As a user, I need a limit on concurrent tool execution goroutines to prevent resource exhaustion.
acceptanceCriteria: In agent.go Run(), when executing tool calls concurrently (the wg/goroutine loop), add a semaphore channel to limit concurrency to at most 5 goroutines
Create a 'sem := make(chan struct{}, 5)' before the loop, acquire before each goroutine starts (sem <- struct{}{}), release when done (<-sem in defer)
If there are 5 or fewer tool calls, behavior is unchanged (all run concurrently)
If there are more than 5, the excess waits for a slot
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add /retry command to re-send the last user message
userStories.description: As a user, when the AI gives a bad response, I want to quickly retry my last message without retyping it.
acceptanceCriteria: Add /retry command to handleCommand in cmd/root.go
Finds the last user message in ag.Messages() (skipping system messages)
Removes the last assistant response (and any tool messages after it) from the conversation
Re-runs ag.Run(ctx, lastUserMessage) with the cleaned history
If no user message found, prints 'No previous message to retry.'
Add /retry to readline autocompleter and /help output
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add /tokens command for per-message token breakdown
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see which messages are consuming the most context so I can manage my conversation size.
acceptanceCriteria: Add /tokens command to handleCommand in cmd/root.go
Iterates through ag.Messages() and estimates tokens per message using tokens.EstimateTokens
Displays a table: index, role (colored), token count, and first 60 chars of content (truncated with ...)
At the bottom, shows total tokens and context usage percentage
Shows the top 5 largest messages highlighted in yellow if they exceed 1000 tokens
Add /tokens to readline autocompleter and /help output
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add /drop command to remove specific messages from context
userStories.description: As a user, I want to selectively remove messages from the conversation to free context space.
acceptanceCriteria: Add /drop command to handleCommand: '/drop N' removes message at index N, '/drop N-M' removes messages N through M inclusive
Does not allow dropping the system message (index 0 if it exists)
After dropping, prints 'Dropped N message(s). Context freed: ~X tokens'
If index is out of range, prints appropriate error
Add /drop to readline autocompleter and /help output
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add configurable timeouts via environment variables
userStories.description: As a user, I want to customize tool timeouts without changing source code.
acceptanceCriteria: Check for BU_BASH_TIMEOUT environment variable in execBash (default: 120 seconds)
Check for BU_HTTP_TIMEOUT environment variable in execHttpRequest (default: 30 seconds)
Check for BU_FETCH_TIMEOUT environment variable in ToolFetchLocalURL and ToolSearchWeb (default: 5 seconds)
Check for BU_TEST_TIMEOUT environment variable in execRunTests (default: 300 seconds)
Parse each as integer seconds, falling back to the existing default if not set or invalid
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Improve auto-compact notification with message preview
userStories.description: As a user, when auto-compact removes messages, I want to know which messages were pruned so I'm not surprised by lost context.
acceptanceCriteria: In agent.go CompactToTokens, before removing messages, collect a summary of what's being removed: the role and first 40 chars of each message being pruned
Change CompactToTokens to return both the count and a string summary of pruned messages
In the Run() loop auto-compact block, print the summary below the existing pruning notification, showing up to 5 removed messages with truncated previews
Format: '  Pruned: [user] "Fix the login bug by..." | [assistant] "I'll update the auth..." | ...'
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implemented: CompactToTokens returns (int, string) with pruned message previews

userStories.title: Add /files command to show all files touched this session
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see a summary of every file the agent has read, written, or edited in this session.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a 'touchedFiles map[string]string' field to the Agent struct (maps file path to last action: 'read', 'write', 'edit')
In executeTool, after successful execution of read_file, write_file, edit_file, or batch_replace, record the file path(s) and action in touchedFiles
Add /files command to handleCommand that displays the touchedFiles map sorted by path, with action colored (read=gray, write=green, edit=yellow)
Add /files to readline autocompleter and /help output
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implemented: touchedFiles map on Agent, trackTouchedFile in executeTool, /files command with sorted colored output
